behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification: The failure risk is at module handoff plus an external side effect: validator -> routing -> DLQ payload. The behavior explicitly requires proving the invalid row is visible in the dead-letter queue with preserved row number (round-trip side-effect visibility), which unit-only tests can miss. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML decision is involved and no multi-agent sequence is described.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline against a real bucket/object and real DLQ endpoint, include rows missing required fields, then read back DLQ messages and assert each contains the original CSV row number and row payload.

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is pure transformation logic (string/date parsing and normalization) and does not require real infra, external contracts, or side-effect readback. Additional higher-level checks add little new reality signal for this specific behavior. Rejected higher levels: the behavior text is limited to conversion correctness, not pipeline wiring, IO, or external services.
behaviors.test_description: Call the date-normalization function with representative ISO 8601, US, and European inputs (including edge cases) and assert canonical output strings.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification: This is a real runtime/resource behavior (timeout and memory pressure) that commonly fails only under OS/runtime reality and full pipeline execution. Unit tests cannot reveal allocator, buffering, backpressure, or process-level memory characteristics at this scale. Rejected higher levels: no model output quality or multi-agent chain is part of the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the pipeline end-to-end on a 500,000-row file under production-like configuration, measure elapsed time and peak memory, and assert completion before timeout with no OOM.

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification: The behavior depends on real external error-path handling from S3 (transient 503) and correct retry/backoff behavior under actual network/service semantics. Mock-only tests can hide SDK/HTTP retry-edge mismatches. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML decision or multi-agent sequence appears in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Trigger real transient 503 responses from S3 (or a real HTTP-compatible endpoint in front of S3 behavior), run a batch, and assert retry attempts follow exponential backoff and the batch proceeds instead of hard-failing.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification: This requires proving an externally visible persistence side effect across restart boundaries: write some rows, restart, then read PostgreSQL and verify idempotency/no duplicates. Unit tests of dedupe logic alone can miss transaction boundaries, commit timing, and restart recovery behavior. Rejected higher levels: behavior does not depend on AI/ML outputs or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run a batch against real PostgreSQL, force a mid-batch restart, resume processing, then query the target table and assert each logical row appears exactly once.
